title: ophrys tenthredinifera var. aprilia
intro: Ophrys tenthredinifera var. aprilia, also known as the Bee Orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. Traditionally, the Bee Orchid is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use and availability are highly restricted due to its rarity and protected status.
